evidence.pageSubtitle: Our approach to mastery, curriculum standards, content safety, and children's privacy.

howWeMeasure.heading: How we measure mastery
howWeMeasure.diagnosticBody: Every child starts with a short diagnostic that captures a baseline across our financial-literacy concept taxonomy — topics like saving, spending, budgeting, investing, and risk. No score is shown to the child; it simply calibrates where to begin.
howWeMeasure.progressChecksBody: After every few learning sessions the app re-measures the same concepts. These short checks let us track movement — whether a concept is emerging, developing, or consolidated — without putting pressure on the learner.
howWeMeasure.taxonomyBody: Every lesson, question, and scenario is tagged to a specific concept in our taxonomy. This means a child's strengths and gaps are always visible at the topic level, not just as a raw score.

standards.heading: Standards alignment
standards.body: Our curriculum is aligned to recognised financial-education frameworks, including the UK Money and Pensions Service (MaPS) financial capability framework and the US Common Core State Standards (CCSS) for financial literacy. Every module carries the relevant framework tags so parents and educators can see exactly which competencies a lesson develops.
standards.note: Framework alignment is reviewed with each new curriculum release.

safety.heading: Safety
safety.aiModerationBody: All AI-generated content passes through an automated moderation layer before it reaches a child. Lessons, feedback, and coaching suggestions are checked for age-appropriateness, accuracy, and tone.
safety.curatedBody: Video and article lessons are curated by the editorial team before publication. Anything flagged by moderation or the curation queue is withheld until reviewed.
safety.designedForKidsBody: InvestiKid is built as a kids' product end-to-end — no advertising, no social feeds, no exposure to external content outside curated educational materials.

privacy.heading: Privacy
privacy.consentBody: Children below the age of digital consent in their country require a parent or guardian to approve their account before they can use the app. Consent is recorded and auditable.
privacy.minimisationBody: We collect only what is necessary to run the app: a username, date of birth, country, and learning progress. We do not collect home addresses, phone numbers, photos, or financial information.
privacy.noExposureHeading: No public exposure of children's data
privacy.noExposureBody: A child's progress and personal details are visible only to them and their linked parent or guardian. Leaderboard features are opt-in and require explicit parent consent, off by default.

privacyNotice.meta: Version {{version}} · Written for learners aged 8 and above
privacyNotice.intro: When you sign up to InvestiKid, we show you this notice and record that you (or your parent or guardian) agreed to it. If we make important changes, we will show you the updated notice and ask you to agree again before you can keep using the app.

whatIsApp.heading: What is InvestiKid?
whatIsApp.body: InvestiKid is a learning app that teaches you about money — how to save it, how to spend it wisely, and how the world of investing works. To do that, we need to know a few things about you so the app works properly and keeps you safe.

whatWeCollect.heading: What information do we collect?
whatWeCollect.usernameDesc: the name you use to log in. It does not have to be your real name.
whatWeCollect.dobDesc: so we know your age and can give you the right level of protection.
whatWeCollect.countryDesc: so we know which rules apply to your data and which currency to show you.
whatWeCollect.passwordDesc: stored in a scrambled form so no one can read it, not even us.
whatWeCollect.emailDesc: only if you are old enough to have one under the rules in your country.
whatWeCollect.parentEmailLabel: A parent or guardian's email address
whatWeCollect.parentEmailDesc: if you are below the age of digital consent, we ask for their permission first.
whatWeCollect.progressDesc: lessons completed, XP earned, level, and streak count.
whatWeCollect.topicsLabel: Which topics you are learning about
whatWeCollect.topicsDesc: so we can show you the right lessons.
whatWeCollect.notCollectedPost: collect your home address, phone number, photo, or information about your family's finances.

whyWeCollect.heading: Why do we collect it?
whyWeCollect.loginSafelyDesc: so you can log in safely.
whyWeCollect.checkAgeDesc: to check your age and keep you safe.
whyWeCollect.countryRulesDesc: to follow the right rules for your country.
whyWeCollect.parentPermissionDesc: to get their permission if you are young.
whyWeCollect.rememberProgressDesc: so the app remembers what you have done.
whyWeCollect.topicChoiceDesc: to show you the right lessons.
whyWeCollect.onlyToRun: We only use your information to run the app and keep it safe. We do not use it to sell you things or to follow you around the internet.

whoCanSee.heading: Who can see your information?
whoCanSee.youDesc: you can always see your own information inside the app.
whoCanSee.parentDesc: if they gave permission for your account, they can see your progress and ask for a copy of your data.
whoCanSee.teamDesc: to keep the app running, fix problems, and make sure it is safe. The team is not allowed to share your information with anyone else.
whoCanSee.noSharePost: share your personal information with advertisers, data brokers, or any other company.

youtube.heading: Lesson videos (YouTube)
youtube.body: Some lessons include educational videos embedded from YouTube. When a video loads, YouTube (a Google service) may receive technical data — such as device and IP information — so it can play the video. We use YouTube's privacy-enhanced mode (youtube-nocookie.com), which does not set tracking cookies unless a video is actually played. We do not share your child's account details with YouTube. Google handles this technical data under its own privacy policy.

retention.heading: How long do we keep your information?
retention.body: We keep your information for as long as you have an account with us.
retention.deletionIntro: If you or your parent or guardian asks us to delete your account:
retention.step1: We close the account straight away. You will not be able to log in.
retention.step2: After 30 days, we delete most of your personal information. We keep your year of birth and country briefly — without your name or email, this cannot identify you.
retention.step3: We keep a record of when you joined and when you asked us to delete your account, because the law requires it.

yourChoices.heading: Your choices
yourChoices.recommendationsDesc: switched off by default. You can turn it on in account settings.
yourChoices.marketingDesc: switched off by default. You can change this in account settings.
yourChoices.deleteAccountDesc: you or your parent or guardian can ask at any time.
yourChoices.seeDataDesc: you can ask for a copy of all the information we hold about you.
yourChoices.fixInfoDesc: if something is wrong, you can update it in your profile settings.
yourChoices.minorNote: If you are below the age limit in your country, your parent or guardian manages these choices for you — to make sure a grown-up is looking out for you.

contact.heading: Contact us
contact.bodyPre: If you have a question about your information, or want to ask for a copy of your data or delete your account, please contact us at
contact.complain: If you are not happy with how we have handled your information, you have the right to complain to the data protection authority in your country — for example, the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) in the UK.
